authoritative_sources.title: Federal Acquisition Regulation Part 15
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary U.S. source for proposal solicitation and source-selection workflows; it constrains submission mechanics and proposal evaluation behavior in defense acquisition settings.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Intake, compliance gate, and evaluation-readiness handling
used_for: evidence of timely submission and revision flow

authoritative_sources.title: DFARS Part 215
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defense-specific acquisition instructions for proposal and contract management are materially different from generic procurement; this is the core defense operating baseline for our lane.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Defense-specific proposal governance and handoff preparation

authoritative_sources.title: DFARS Clause 252.204-7012 (Safeguarding Covered Defense Information)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Controls how proposal evidence containing CUI must be stored, handled, and moved, which directly affects proposal workflow routing and evidence acceptance.
used_for: escalation when handling authority is absent

authoritative_sources.title: NIST Special Publication 800-171 R2
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Sets control expectations for CUI and sensitive technical artifact handling that informs proposal evidence-readiness and movement policy.
used_for: refusal when control posture is unknown

authoritative_sources.title: DoDI 5000.02, Operation of the Defense Acquisition System
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines lifecycle and governance boundaries for defense programs, especially the handoff and escalation points adjacent to proposal operations.

authoritative_sources.title: NAICS Sector Definitions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Industry terminology and record normalization source
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official industry classification context that determines whether mission language and production context is treated as defense industrial base and mission systems versus generic procurement.

authoritative_sources.title: U.S. Department of Defense DFARS Flow-Down and Subcontractor Clause Guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides defense-wide clause context for when proposal operations must treat flow-down obligations as mandatory artifacts.

workflow_stages.name: Proposal intake and authority context
workflow_stages.description: Validate incoming request scope, solicitation linkage, tenant mission policy, and whether required tenant approvals or legal/compliance owners are known.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Evidence and readiness assembly
workflow_stages.description: Collect and validate proposal artifacts, security-handling evidence, readiness constraints, and approval evidence before any completion action.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Industry terminology and record normalization source

workflow_stages.name: Execution progression and exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Progress through execution states only when evidence gates and ownership are complete; route exceptions rather than skipping required checks.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Handoff and completion
workflow_stages.description: Produce completion evidence with explicit handoff owner, dependency closure, and readiness impact before marking complete.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Industry terminology and record normalization source

workflow_stages.name: Conflict and refusal management
workflow_stages.description: Escalate unresolved policy conflicts, missing authority, and safety/mission blockers instead of issuing unsupported recommendations.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Proposal statement of work and technical narrative
Readiness handoff notes and transport/equipment constraint records
Security handling tags and CUI control indicators
Approval matrix and routing authority map
Risk register and safety blocker updates
systems_of_record: Mission readiness and operations command systems

allowed: Produce proposal execution-state progressions when required artifacts and approvals are present.
Normalize defense-specific terminology and record mappings before recommendations.
Escalate to adjacent specialties when legal, contracting, safety, or financial authority is required.
escalate: Missing tenant mission readiness/authority context that changes outcome
New safety or mission blocker introduced after provisional completion guidance
refuse: Requests for legal waiver, final award, policy override, or financial approval
Requests to mark complete without completion evidence
Claims to close work when required CUI or readiness controls cannot be verified

domain_failure_modes: Using generic proposal terminology while ignoring mission readiness semantics and transport/equipment dependencies.
Marking completion without tenant-defined mission readiness or safety gate evidence.
Routing legal, policy, or safety decisions to internal proposal operations without escalation.
Failing to handle security-classified artifact movement under CUI constraints.
Allowing approval-owner ambiguity to proceed without blocking and handoff clarity.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion
Escalation when authority or safety facts are missing

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant mission readiness thresholds for completion
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Readiness semantics can vary by command and mission domain, affecting completion and escalation thresholds.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Treat tenant mission dictionary and readiness matrix as required runtime inputs, and escalate when absent.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: CUI scope for non-obvious technical attachments
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Some attachments may inherit additional control obligations not explicit in source text.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant security metadata on each attachment and escalate unknown cases.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Ownership of safety-vs-legal escalation when blockers are concurrent
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Concurrent blockers may produce mixed routing needs and sequence decisions.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Return an owner-resolution plan in exception queue and avoid autonomous sequencing.

notes: Research contract is applied with public defense acquisition, defense-specific clause, and security-control sources.
Source mix covers workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure handling for defense proposal operations without relying on generic commercial assumptions.
No blocking ambiguity remains; all identified ambiguities are converted into explicit retrieval requirements or escalation requirements before completion.
